nameMeanings.name: Gernon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Grennan   1.

variants.name: Grennan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Kieran.
English (Lancashire, of Norman origin): nickname for a man with a moustache, from Old French gernon, grenon ‘moustache’.
Irish (Offaly, Westmeath and Down): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Grianáin (see Greenan ).

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Ralph Gernon II was born in 1152, in Stansted Village, Essex, England, United Kingdom, his father, Ralph Girnon, was 41 and his mother, Maud de St. Valery de Braose, was 42. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Isabel Basset. He died in 1247, in Bakewell, Derbyshire, England, at the age of 95.
